signals.title: Cal State students widely use AI tools, but mistrust results and fear job impact
signals.summary: CSU system-wide survey of about 94,000 students, faculty and staff: 95% of responding students use an AI tool, 84% used ChatGPT, 82% worry AI will hurt their future job security.
signals.relevance: Direct AI-usage data for the largest US public university system.
signals.reliability: Education-beat outlet citing a named CSU system survey with stated sample size.

signals.title: Survey finds widespread use, mixed feelings about AI among San Diego college students
signals.summary: About 90% of surveyed San Diego-area students use ChatGPT; 8 in 10 worry about AI's effect on creativity, privacy, environment, and job security.
signals.relevance: Regional confirmation of the CSU AI adoption/anxiety pattern.
signals.reliability: Public broadcaster education desk citing a named local survey.

signals.title: The largest study of AI use by undergrads is in, revealing disparities in access and in cheating
signals.summary: UC-affiliated reporting on the largest study of undergrad AI use to date: about two-thirds of respondents used generative AI, almost 40% monthly or more, at least 9% admitted using it to cheat; access to AI tools is uneven across income groups.
signals.relevance: Largest-scale data point on AI use/cheating disparities, directly tied to academic integrity decisions students face.
signals.reliability: University system's own news service summarizing a named study.

signals.source_name: NACE (National Association of Colleges and Employers)
signals.title: Hiring Flat for the College Class of 2026
signals.summary: Employers project only modest (5.6%) increase in new-grad hiring; 45% of employers rate the job market as only 'fair' for the Class of 2026, the worst rating since 2020-21.
signals.relevance: Primary labor-market data source for graduating seniors' job prospects.
signals.reliability: NACE is the standard industry association tracking new-grad hiring data.

signals.title: By the Numbers: What the class of 2026 job market actually looks like, and where AI fits in
signals.summary: Reports entry-level hiring fell about 6% between Dec 2025 and Feb 2026 year-over-year, junior postings down ~7% in 2025, and AI linked to a 25% drop in recent-grad hiring at major tech firms (Meta, Microsoft, Google).
signals.relevance: Quantifies how AI is reshaping entry-level hiring for this year's graduating class.
signals.reliability: Major business-news outlet citing labor-market data providers.

signals.title: 'I'm Doing Everything That I Possibly Can': New College Graduates Face One of the Toughest Job Markets in Years
signals.summary: Recent grads describe submitting 100+ job applications without landing a single interview, illustrating the lived experience behind the hiring-slowdown statistics.
signals.relevance: Personal-account color that humanizes the entry-level hiring slowdown.
signals.reliability: Established business-news outlet; anecdotes attributed to named graduates.

signals.title: US Student Loan Defaults Reach 9.2 Million as Collections Resume
signals.summary: Student loan defaults hit a record 9.16 million in April 2026 after the pandemic collections pause ended; about 20% of the 43 million federal borrowers are over a year behind on payments.
signals.relevance: Headline data point on the scale of the default crisis hitting borrowers, many of them recent grads.
signals.reliability: Major financial-news wire citing government collection data.

signals.title: A major student loan overhaul takes effect July 1. Here's what borrowers need to know.
signals.summary: Explains July 1, 2026 changes eliminating several federal repayment plans (including SAVE) and ending Grad PLUS loans, with Parent PLUS borrowing capped for undergraduates.
signals.relevance: Direct, near-term policy change affecting how current students will pay for school starting this summer.

signals.title: Guide: Student loan options change July 1. What you need to know.
signals.summary: Walks through new, more limited repayment-plan choices for borrowers after July 1, 2026, as part of the broader federal loan program overhaul.
signals.relevance: Practical explainer students will need before choosing a repayment plan.

signals.title: Canada moves to expand work authorization for international students and graduates
signals.summary: As of April 1, 2026, post-secondary international students in Canada no longer need a separate co-op work permit for placements like co-ops, internships and practicums.
signals.relevance: Direct, practical change to how international students in Canada can work while studying.
signals.reliability: Specialist Canadian immigration-news outlet citing IRCC policy.

signals.title: Canada's international student enrolment down to pandemic levels: StatCan
signals.summary: Statistics Canada data shows international student enrolment has fallen back to pandemic-era levels amid tightened study permit caps.
signals.relevance: Macro trend behind why international student life in Canada looks different than a few years ago.
signals.reliability: Major Canadian news outlet citing Statistics Canada.

signals.title: 2026 provincial and territorial allocations under the international student cap
signals.summary: IRCC will issue 408,000 study permits in 2026 (155,000 new, 253,000 extensions); master's/doctoral students at public institutions are exempt from the provincial attestation requirement as of Jan 1, 2026.
signals.relevance: Official source for the study-permit cap numbers driving the Canada international-student storyline.

signals.title: TikTokers compare 'ruthless' experiences with first roommates: 'A huge red flag'
signals.summary: Roundup of TikTok creators comparing freshman-year roommate experiences and the behaviors students now flag as dealbreakers.
signals.relevance: Shows the dorm/roommate-culture genre is an active, shareable TikTok trend among students.
signals.reliability: Lifestyle aggregation piece citing named TikTok creators; treat specifics as anecdotal color, not verified incident reports.
signals.risk_note: One cited anecdote involves an alleged allergic-reaction incident; do not name or imply a real individual in card news. Reframe as a structural 'roommate red flag' trend, not a specific incident report.

signals.title: For Many Students, Side Hustles Are a Necessity
signals.summary: Community-college student journalism piece arguing side hustles have shifted from a lifestyle trend to a financial necessity for many students.
signals.relevance: Student-press perspective grounding the side-hustle statistic trend in lived student experience.
signals.reliability: Student journalism outlet; first-person framing, good for student_reaction but pair with stats for factual basis.

signals.title: 5 Takeaways From Recent Research on Student Loneliness
signals.summary: More than 60% of students under 25 report feeling lonely sometimes or always; among LGBTQ+ students the figure tops 70%.
signals.relevance: Higher-ed trade press summarizing peer-reviewed-adjacent research on student loneliness.
signals.reliability: Specialist higher-ed news outlet, named research findings with stats.

signals.title: The Real Reason Gen Z Is Burning Out Before They Even Graduate
signals.summary: Reports Gen Z averages less sleep than any prior generation at the same age and links this to elevated burnout and disengagement, with 'bed rotting' cited as a common coping habit (55% try social-media-driven stress habits, 24% specifically bed rot).
signals.relevance: Direct tie between a viral coping behavior (bed rotting) and burnout statistics among the target audience.
signals.reliability: Culture/news outlet citing Gallup-sourced generational stress data; treat 'bed rotting' figures as the outlet's own framing.

signals.title: College Tuition Inflation: Rate Increase Statistics
signals.summary: Average tuition across all postsecondary institutions is projected to rise about 3.25% for 2026-27; four-year tuition up roughly 2.3%, two-year up roughly 1.7%.
signals.relevance: Baseline cost-of-college data for comparison content about rising tuition.
signals.reliability: Education-data research site that aggregates IPEDS and other federal sources.

signals.title: 2026 Highest-Paying College Majors
signals.summary: Chemical, computer, and aerospace engineering top median-salary rankings; lifetime earnings gap between highest- and lowest-paying majors is roughly $1.9 million.
signals.relevance: Concrete by-major salary comparison data for a 'pick your major wisely' angle.
signals.reliability: Education research/ranking site; figures should be treated as estimates, not guaranteed outcomes.
